items.title:
Did you know that in 2015, Israel banned Heinz from calling its product "ketchup"? 🍅🚫🇮🇱
It started when their biggest competitor, Osem, used an old legal standard to claim Heinz didn't meet the definition of ketchup. The authorities agreed, and Heinz had a massive branding crisis.
They needed a new name in Hebrew, but there wasn't a good everyday word for "condiment." So they dug into the dictionary and found an obscure word invented in the 1950s by a linguist who hated foreign words: Mitbal (מִתְבָּל).
The word had been a total failure for decades—no one used it. But for a whole year, Heinz had no choice but to put "Tomato Mitbal" on their bottles.
The law eventually changed back, but a dead word got a second life thanks to corporate condiment warfare.
#Heinz #Ketchup #Israel #Hebrew #Linguistics #FoodHistory #Mitbal #Osem #MarketingFacts #TelAviv

items.title:
Tai Brown aka Tainika Brown tried to extort me by threatening to file a false complaint against me to the Maryland Board of Nursing if I did NOT hand over my intellectual property to her… We are industry competitors. Both providing post op education to professionals, but only one of us is licensed and only one of us is a nurse. 

This is abuse of the regulatory process and extortion. 

#nursesoftiktok
